title: ficus philippinensis f. setibracteata
intro: Ficus philippinensis f. setibracteata, also known as the Philippine banyan, is a variant of the Ficus philippinensis tree species, characterized by its unique setibracteate leaves with leaflets connected by a petiole. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Ficus philippinensis f. setibracteata include reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve boiling or decocting the leaves or bark in water to create teas, infusions, or tonics for medicinal use.
